title: The Scaffold Programme
text:
The Scaffold Programme - SupportGoWhere
The Scaffold Programme
Share link
Supports Secondary 1 and 2 students, who have been identified to have higher needs, including socio-emotional and behavioural difficulties, at risk of delinquency, and observed to have low school connectedness.
SERVICE HIGHLIGHTS
Centre-based
Free
Referral required
SERVICE PROVIDERS
Find service providers near you
Collapse all sections
About this support

The Scaffold Programme (TSP) is an upstream, early intervention programme that supports lower secondary school students in mainstream schools, who are predisposed to latent risk factors or premature withdrawal from school.

TSP aims to provide quality school social work intervention for students and their ecosystems that lead to improved school outcomes, reduced referrals to downstream programmes, improvement in parent-child relationships and school-student attachment.

Who is this for?

Secondary 1 and 2 students who have been identified to have higher needs including:

Socio-emotional and behavioural difficulties

At risk of delinquency

Observed to have low school connectedness

What to expect?

The one year programme is available in selected partner schools

Agency will have a discussion with relevant school personnel on the needs of the classes

The agency will then customise the service component to address the unique needs of the class

How to apply?

The programme adopts a class-based design – i.e. school teachers will identify and refer suitable classes to join the programme at the start of the calendar year

Information last updated on 29 Sep 2025
